---
title: "Research"
toc: false
---
An overview of my past and current research programs organized into four main areas:

::: {.card-grid}

::: {.research-card}
![](affect-dynamics/metrics.png){.card-img-top}

### [Affect Dynamics](affect-dynamics/index.qmd)
How we shift between positive and negative emotional states is related to our psychological well-being. Understanding what causes these transitions can help us develop tailored therapeutic interventions to improve mental health.

[Learn more →](affect-dynamics/index.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary .btn-sm}
:::

::: {.research-card}
![](scholarly-communication/replacing.png){.card-img-top}

### [Scholarly Communication](scholarly-communication/index.qmd)
Academic knowledge creation and communication has been hijacked by large commercial publishers. We develop models and tools for scholar-governed publishing using existing public infrastructure.

[Learn more →](scholarly-communication/index.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary .btn-sm}
:::


::: {.research-card}
![](exercise-brain-cognition/exercise.png){.card-img-top}

### [Exercise, Brain & Cognition](exercise-brain-cognition/index.qmd)
Does physical exercise improve cognition? We measure neurophysiological signals during physical exercise to investigate exercise-brain links.

[Learn more →](exercise-brain-cognition/index.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary .btn-sm}
:::


::: {.research-card}
![](hep/hep.png){.card-img-top}

### [Heartbeat-Evoked Potential](exercise-brain-cognition/index.qmd)
The heartbeat-evoked potential (HEP) is a neural signal that reflects the brain's processing of cardiac signals. We investigate its psychophysiological origins and its relation to physical exercise and interoception.

[Learn more →](exercise-brain-cognition/index.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary .btn-sm}
:::

:::
